input.background: Suppose the life of a certain security alarm system is exponential distribution with constant failure rate λ = 0.00008 failures per hour.
input.question: What is the probability that a security system will last at least 10,000 h?

output.method: 事件发生概率及独立性
output.answer:
The probability that a security system will last at least $10,000 \mathrm{~h}$ is

$$
P\{T \geq 10,000\}=e^{-\lambda t} e^{-(0.00008)(10,000)}=e^{-0.8}=0.4493
$$

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 2: Distribution Functions and Its Applications
